2606.28270.title: Agent-Native Immune System: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Engineering
2606.28270.paper_summary: The transition from static chat bots to autonomous agents--equipped with persistent memory, tool-use protocols, and multi-agent collaboration--has fundamentally expanded the AI threat landscape. Current defense mechanisms, such as perimeter security and training-time alignment, remain external to the agent's active reasoning loop. Consequently, they fall short: a fully aligned agent remains highly vulnerable to runtime hijacking via memory poisoning, tool-chain manipulation, or multi-agent protocol attacks. To address this critical gap, we introduce the Agent-Native Immune System (ANIS), the first biologically inspired, endogenous defense architecture embedded directly within the agent's cognitive loop. Our framework presents four primary contributions. First, we design a six-layer Immune Tower (L0-L5), distinctly incorporating Barrier Immunity (L1) as a non-cognitive, physical-and-logical isolation layer. Second, we establish a unified taxonomy of Agent Viruses and Agent Vaccines, formalizing the critical distinction between superficial non-parametric defenses and robust parametric vaccines. Third, we conceptualize the Harness Triad--Meta, Self, and Auto--a self-monitoring, meta-cognitive automation backbone that drives Continual Immune Learning (CIL), enabling vaccines to dynamically adapt to novel threats. Finally, we establish a rigorous theoretical demarcation between model alignment and agent immunity: while alignment provides a static "constitutional" value foundation during training, ANIS serves as the dynamic "law enforcement" mechanism during runtime. We conclude by framing open challenges for the field, including immune protocol standardization, novel evaluation metrics such as the Autoimmunity Rate (false-positive intervention rate), and the co-evolutionary dynamics between pathogens and vaccines within collective intelligence ecosystems.
2606.28270.paper_summary_zh: 靜態聊天機器人向自主代理的轉變——配備持久記憶、工具使用協議和多代理協作——根本上擴大了人工智慧的威脅範圍。當前的防禦機制，如周邊安全和訓練時的對齊，仍然是外部於代理的主動推理循環。因此，它們無法滿足需求：一個完全對齊的代理仍然高度容易受到運行時劫持，通過記憶中毒、工具鏈操控或多代理協議攻擊。為了解決這一關鍵缺口，我們引入了代理原生免疫系統（ANIS），這是第一個生物啟發的內源性防禦架構，直接嵌入代理的認知循環中。我們的框架提出了四個主要貢獻。首先，我們設計了一個六層免疫塔（L0-L5），明確地將屏障免疫（L1）納入作為一個非認知的物理和邏輯隔離層。其次，我們建立了一個統一的代理病毒和代理疫苗分類法，正式區分了表面非參數防禦和穩健的參數疫苗之間的關鍵區別。第三，我們概念化了控制三元組——元、自我和自動——一個自我監控的元認知自動化骨幹，推動持續免疫學習（CIL），使疫苗能夠動態適應新威脅。最後，我們建立了一個嚴謹的理論劃分，區分模型對齊和代理免疫：雖然對齊在訓練期間提供了一個靜態的「憲法」價值基礎，ANIS則在運行時作為動態的「執法」機制。我們通過框架化該領域的開放挑戰作為結論，包括免疫協議標準化、新的評估指標如自體免疫率（假陽性干預率），以及病原體與疫苗在集體智慧生態系統中的共同演化動態。
2606.28270.author: Bo Shen et.al.
2606.28270.authors: Bo Shen, Lifeng Chang, Tianyuan Wei, Yunpeng Li, Feng Shi, Yichen Han, Peijie Gao, Shiyi Kuang, Xin Chang, Dehui Li
